title: Hakea microstyla
taxonomy: Hakea microstyla is classified within the family Proteaceae. This family is a group of flowering plant species that are primarily native to the southern hemisphere. Hakea microstyla itself is a species of shrub that is endemic to Western Australia. As a member of the Proteaceae family, it is closely related to other similar species such as Grevillea and Banksia. The classification of Hakea microstyla is further narrowed down within the genus Hakea, which consists of over 150 species of shrubs and trees.

Traditional healers in Australia have used Hakea species for various purposes, including treating skin conditions and wounds. The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the health benefits of Hakea microstyla.
